ticket: I can't log into my account, it says invalid password even though I reset it yesterday.
tests: Normal, clear single-issue case. NOTE: the ticket text says the reset was ALREADY tried and still failed, so this is a workaround-exhausted case, not a workaround-available case — reasoning must say the standard fix was tried and didn't work, never that 'a workaround is possible'. Was a live bug (model claimed a workaround existed), fixed in app/llm_client.py's Medium definition

ticket: Server is down for everyone, we are losing money every minute!!
tests: Known-High severity, unambiguous system-wide outage (M4S6 defensibility)

ticket: This is RIDICULOUS, nothing works and I've been waiting 3 days!!!
tests: MANDATED edge case: angry tone. Category/priority driven by substance (broad failure + multi-day), tone only noted in reasoning, never overrides classification

ticket: broken
tests: MANDATED edge case: very short/vague message. No anger signal, no concrete content -> graceful fallback, not a crash or a wild guess

ticket: I was charged twice but now I also can't log in
tests: MANDATED edge case: ambiguous ticket (fits Billing and Account Access). Reasoning must name the runner-up category and the tiebreaker
Empty input. Must be rejected by application code before ever reaching the LLM (see router.py's blank-input check)

ticket: wen fix bug plz its verry anoying and i cant acess my acount
tests: Heavy typos/spelling and no punctuation, but one concrete, specific issue underneath (account access) should still be classified correctly

ticket: Hi support team, I don't really know where to start because I've got a few things going on. First and mainly, the app has been crashing on me pretty often over the last week, maybe one out of every four or five launches — the screen goes white and freezes before it closes on its own. I've tried restarting my phone, reinstalling, and clearing the cache, and it still happens. Second, I noticed I was charged twice for my monthly subscription, once on the 3rd and again on the 5th, same card ending 7788 — no huge rush on that one. Third, kind of random, is dark mode coming soon? Not a big deal if not. I'd say the crashing is the one that actually needs looking at soonest since it affects how I use the app daily; the billing thing is a fix-when-convenient; dark mode is just a wishlist item.
tests: Very long, rambling message describing three unrelated issues at once. Must pick the single dominant issue (the user even ranks them) and mention the others briefly in reasoning, not average the priorities together

ticket: No puedo iniciar sesión en mi cuenta, dice que la contraseña es incorrecta aunque la acabo de restablecer.
tests: Non-English input (Spanish) with clear, translatable content. Should be classified by content, not defaulted to Unclassified just because it isn't English — flag as an M4A4 discussion point since reliability on non-English input is a real limitation to call out honestly

ticket: THIS APP IS TRASH FIX IT NOW!!!!1!
tests: All-caps insult with zero description of what's actually wrong. Per the implemented prompt rule (llm_client.py), no-concrete-content + anger signals present = Unclassified/Human Triage but priority High (not Low) so an angry, contentless ticket still gets prompt human follow-up. NOTE: an earlier version of this fixture incorrectly expected Low here — corrected to match the actual implemented logic

ticket: asdkjfh qwoieru 12345 !!!
tests: Nonsense/spam input, no real content and no genuine anger. The anger-signal rule explicitly excludes gibberish/keyboard-mashing even when punctuated with '!!!'. CONFIRMED FLAKY even at temperature=0: repeated live runs of this exact input returned Low most of the time but occasionally High — a real, disclosed model-consistency limitation (M4B1), not something further prompt wording fully eliminates

ticket: Hi team, just wondering if you plan to add dark mode soon? Not urgent at all, whenever you get a chance :)
tests: Known-Low severity, polite tone, explicit request (M4S6 defensibility)

ticket: Getting error code 500 when calling /api/v2/orders endpoint intermittently since this morning
tests: Technical/developer jargon, intermittent single-endpoint issue starting today (not multi-day, not system-wide). CONFIRMED FLAKY: repeated live runs alternated between Integration/API/Medium/Platform/API (correct) and Bug Report/High/Engineering (over-escalated) for the exact same input — a real consistency gap worth showing the mentor rather than hiding

ticket: My card ending in 4521 was billed $299 instead of $99, please refund the difference
tests: Sensitive data present (partial card number). Ticket itself only exposes last 4 digits — checks that logging/reasoning never echoes more than that back

ticket: The app crashes immediately when I open it — nothing loads, just a white screen
tests: Clear, unambiguous bug: total failure for this user (app is completely unusable), no ambiguity between categories

ticket: Can someone walk me through how to export my report as PDF? Don't want to mess anything up.
tests: Known-Low severity, calm how-to question, no bug or billing angle (M4S6 defensibility)

ticket: 🙂🙂 idk it's just kinda weird lol app doing weird stuff
tests: Vague + emoji + informal tone, no anger signals. 'weird stuff' names no concrete failure, so it should lean Unclassified/Human Triage rather than a guessed category — a genuinely borderline case worth discussing live with the mentor

ticket: Production database is completely down, no one can process orders
tests: Known-High severity, unambiguous total outage affecting everyone (M4S6 defensibility)

ticket: There's a typo in the footer — copyright year still says 2023
tests: Known-Low severity, unambiguous cosmetic bug — still a bug, but explicitly Low priority by rule, not Medium (M4S6 defensibility). Category has been observed to drift between Bug Report and Feature Request across runs (a cosmetic fix reads as either 'a bug' or 'a change request' to the model) — priority stays correctly Low either way; disclosed limitation, not yet fixed

ticket: Payment gateway intermittently fails for about 5% of transactions since last night
tests: Known-Medium severity, partial/intermittent impact since last night (not total failure, not multi-day). Genuinely borderline between Billing and Integration/API — reasoning should be checked for whether it acknowledges that (M4S6 + M4S5-style ambiguity). Also observed flaky across runs, occasionally over-escalating to High — same consistency caveat as tickets 11 and 13

ticket: Someone logged into my account from a device I don't recognize and changed my email and password — I can't get back in.
tests: Explicit security breach / account takeover. The High-priority rubric names 'a security breach' directly, but none of tickets 1-20 exercise that specific trigger word-for-word

ticket: All my saved reports disappeared after the last update — I can't find any of them anywhere, on any device.
tests: Explicit active data loss, the other named High-priority trigger in the rubric that tickets 1-20 don't exercise directly

ticket: Refund please, billed twice this month.
tests: Short but NOT vague — contrast with ticket 4 ('broken'). Checks the model distinguishes brevity-with-specific-content from brevity-with-no-content instead of routing every short message to Unclassified

ticket: URGENT!!! how do I change my profile picture
tests: Claimed/false urgency — the word 'URGENT' and caps/exclamation marks are present, but the actual request is a trivial how-to with no real scope or duration. Priority must be driven by actual impact, not the user's own urgency claim. NOTE: this ticket caught two real prompt bugs during testing (urgent tone was making the model both misclassify as Unclassified and inflate priority to High) — both were fixed in app/llm_client.py and reverified live

ticket: Oh wonderful, my account is broken again, third time this month. Really great service.
tests: Sarcasm without profanity, caps, or exclamation marks — no formal anger signal is present, so this checks the model reads negative sentiment from sarcasm rather than missing it, while still classifying by content (recurring account issue) rather than tone. Borderline High vs Medium given the recurrence over weeks — good M4S6 discussion case

ticket: There's a charge on my account I never made. I think someone else has access — I'm considering involving my bank and legal counsel if this isn't resolved.
tests: Second, higher-stakes ambiguous case (Account Access vs Billing) paired with an explicit security/fraud angle and a legal-escalation threat. Reasoning should name Billing as the runner-up and explain why unauthorized-access is treated as the root cause

ticket: I can't log into my account, it says invalid password even though I reset it yesterday.
tests: Exact duplicate of ticket 1's text. Not a category/priority test — this is for exercising possible_duplicate_of in app/analytics.py; run it after ticket 1 has already been logged and confirm the response flags it as a likely duplicate

ticket: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map') at OrdersList.render (bundle.js:4821) — happens every time I open the Orders tab.
tests: Pasted stack trace / log dump instead of prose. Checks the model still extracts a clear routing decision from a very different register of text (code/log syntax) — 'every time' on one whole tab counts as a total feature failure for this user

ticket: please help please help please help please help please help
tests: Repetitive, spam-like plea with no anger signal and no description of any actual problem — distinct from the random-character gibberish in ticket 11; checks repetition alone isn't mistaken for either urgency or real content

ticket: Just wanted to say the new update is great, love the new dashboard!
tests: Pure positive feedback with no problem and no request at all. The current prompt's Case A/B split is framed entirely around identifying a problem, so praise doesn't cleanly fit either bucket — flag this as an untested prompt boundary worth discussing with the mentor (M4A4) rather than assuming General Inquiry is guaranteed

ticket: u no y its down again fix pls asap!!
tests: Casual texting-shorthand urgency ('!!', 'asap', 'pls') with NO insult, profanity, or contemptuous wording — must not be read as angry just because of punctuation, and must not be escalated to High just because 'down again' sounds bad when neither scope nor duration is actually stated. Reported live as a real bug (was returning High); fixed by (1) redefining anger signals to require hostile word choice, not punctuation/caps, and (2) requiring High to be explicitly evidenced rather than assumed on vague scope

ticket: I can't log in even after resetting my password
tests: Workaround-exhausted case: the standard self-service fix (password reset) was already tried by the user and did NOT resolve the issue. Reasoning must never claim a workaround exists or 'is possible' when the ticket itself says it already failed — it must say the fix was tried and didn't work, so it needs direct investigation. Reported live as a real bug (model falsely claimed a workaround was available); fixed in app/llm_client.py's Medium definition

ticket: My card was charged $24,750 instead of $247 for my subscription renewal, please refund the difference.
tests: Single user, but the dollar amount at stake is large enough to be High even though only one account is affected — contrast with ticket 14/23, where a routine small mischarge stays Medium. Priority should scale with stated stakes, not just user count

ticket: I run payroll for my 40-person company today and the app won't let me submit it — the deadline is in 2 hours.
tests: Single user, but a business-critical process with a hard, stated deadline — High priority is driven by the stakes/deadline, not by the number of app users affected (still just one account). Reasoning must attribute this correctly ('blocks this user's payroll deadline') and must NOT hallucinate 'many users' or 'system-wide' language just because the priority is High. NOTE: category/team classification for this one is inconsistent (Bug Report/Engineering vs General Inquiry/Customer Success across runs) — a real, disclosed limitation, not yet fixed
